support_evidence: - The lateral cutaneous nerve and the peroneal nerve can be damaged with extended pressure causing a numb patch. Monitor sensation on the leg when tying and be mindful of the amount of time spent in this tie. Check out Day 51 for more information on this topic.

active_answer: Inspect its lashings or welds because rotational force can cause shearing.
active_question: What safety inspection does Rope365 recommend for a center-weighted tripod, and why?
edited_answer: Rope365 recommends inspecting a center-weighted tripod’s lashings or welds because rotational force can cause shearing.
edited_question: What safety inspection does Rope365 recommend for a center-weighted tripod, and why?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement turns the source-supported imperative into a complete attributed answer while retaining the inspected components and shearing rationale.
reason: The replacement turns the source-supported imperative into a complete attributed answer while retaining the inspected components and shearing rationale.
support_evidence: Tripods also have a unique weakness you should pay attention to. The rotational force of a center-weighted load can cause shearing, similar to twisting the top off of a bottle of soda. You should regularly inspect the lashing/welding of any center-weighted tripod for this reason, and this isa big reason why many of the steel-topper providers have moved to a more distributed mounting pattern.
